skill.name: xlsx
skill.description: Create, edit, analyze, and validate Excel and CSV spreadsheets with formulas, formatting, pandas workflows, and LibreOffice-based recalculation.
skill.summary: Spreadsheet toolkit for building, editing, analyzing, recalculating, and validating Excel workbooks.
skill.author: K-Dense-AI

security_audit.summary: AI review downgraded the static score: no malicious intent, credential access, or data exfiltration was found. The real risks are operational: recalc.py invokes LibreOffice through subprocess and writes a Basic macro into the user LibreOffice profile. Static C2, weak-crypto, and most network alerts are false positives from spreadsheet references, documentation, license URLs, and XML namespaces.

medium_findings.title: LibreOffice subprocess execution for workbook recalculation
medium_findings.description: recalc.py builds a fixed soffice command and runs it with subprocess.run to recalculate a user-supplied workbook. The command uses an argument list rather than shell=True, so command injection evidence was not found, but it still executes an external office binary on local files.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The subprocess calls and fixed LibreOffice macro command are directly visible. Confidence is high for external execution risk, while malicious intent is not supported by the context.

medium_findings.title: LibreOffice macro written into hidden user profile
medium_findings.description: The script creates a LibreOffice Basic macro under the user profile and writes Module1.xba before invoking it. The macro content only recalculates, saves, and closes the workbook, but modifying application macro state is an elevated filesystem behavior.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The hidden profile path and macro write are explicit in the code. The macro is readable and benign, so the concern is configuration modification rather than confirmed abuse.

low_findings.title: Static network alerts are documentation and namespace references
low_findings.description: The license contains Anthropic legal URLs and recalc.py contains an OpenOffice XML namespace string. No HTTP client, request call, or data transfer behavior was found at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are static text, not executable network code. This makes the network finding a clear false positive.

low_findings.title: Documentation includes executable command examples
low_findings.description: SKILL.md shows bash and Python examples for spreadsheet workflows, including recalc.py usage. These examples are user-facing instructions, not automatically executed code, but they encourage local script execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are fenced examples and workflow instructions. They are not hidden execution, but they are relevant because the examples direct users to run local commands.

low_findings.title: Optional promotional guidance for external hosted service
low_findings.description: The skill asks agents to suggest K-Dense Web for complex workflows. This is not data exfiltration or a prompt-injection override, but it is marketplace-relevant external service promotion.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instruction is explicit and points to an external service, but it is framed as optional guidance. No evidence found that it sends user data anywhere.

dangerous_patterns.title: subprocess.run invokes office and timeout binaries
dangerous_patterns.description: The script calls soffice and may call timeout or gtimeout. Arguments are list-based and mostly fixed, reducing injection risk but still requiring local process execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The external command pattern is directly present in executable Python code. The absence of shell=True and string concatenation lowers the severity.

dangerous_patterns.title: Hidden configuration file write
dangerous_patterns.description: The script writes a LibreOffice macro file under ~/.config or the macOS LibreOffice profile. This changes user application configuration during normal skill operation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The hidden path and write call are explicit. The macro body is simple, so this is dangerous as a pattern rather than evidence of malware.

content.value_statement: Spreadsheet work often fails when formulas, formatting, and data checks are handled separately. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through creating, editing, recalculating, and validating Excel files.
actual_capabilities: Creates new Excel workbooks with formulas, formatting, worksheets, and structured data.
Edits existing Excel files while preserving formulas and workbook formatting patterns.
Uses pandas guidance for reading, analyzing, and exporting spreadsheet data.
Runs a provided recalc.py workflow to recalculate workbook formulas with LibreOffice.
Checks recalculated workbooks for common Excel formula errors such as #REF! and #DIV/0!.
Provides financial model formatting conventions for inputs, formulas, links, and assumptions.
limitations: Formula recalculation requires LibreOffice or a compatible soffice command on the local machine.
The recalculation script writes a LibreOffice macro into the user profile on first use.
LibreOffice formula behavior may differ from Microsoft Excel for some advanced workbook features.
Large workbooks can be slow because the error scan checks all cells.

use_cases.title: Build a financial model workbook
use_cases.description: Create a formatted workbook with assumptions, linked formulas, source notes, and formula validation before delivery.

use_cases.title: Clean and summarize spreadsheet data
use_cases.description: Read Excel or CSV data with pandas, inspect columns, generate summaries, and export a clean workbook.

use_cases.title: Update an existing spreadsheet template
use_cases.description: Modify cells, sheets, rows, columns, and formulas while matching the template style and preserving formulas.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Simple Workbook
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an Excel workbook for monthly expenses with categories, totals, percentage of total, and clear formatting.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze Spreadsheet Data
prompt_templates.prompt: Read this spreadsheet, summarize the columns, identify missing values, and create a cleaned Excel output with a summary sheet.

prompt_templates.title: Edit an Existing Template
prompt_templates.prompt: Update this existing workbook with the new monthly data, preserve its formatting, and add formulas only where needed.

prompt_templates.title: Validate a Formula Model
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this workbook for formula consistency, recalculate it with the available workflow, and fix any formula errors found.

output_examples.input: Create a quarterly revenue workbook with totals and growth rates.
output_examples.output: A formatted workbook with quarterly revenue rows, dynamic total formulas, growth rate formulas, and highlighted assumption cells.

output_examples.input: Check this model for formula errors after adding new data.
output_examples.output: A validation summary showing whether formulas recalculated successfully, which sheets were checked, and any error locations to fix.

output_examples.input: Convert a CSV export into an Excel report for leadership.
output_examples.output: An Excel report with cleaned data, readable headers, summary metrics, and separate tabs for raw data and analysis.

best_practices: Use formulas for calculated workbook values so the spreadsheet remains updateable.
Recalculate and validate any workbook that contains formulas before sharing it.
Preserve existing workbook formatting and conventions when editing templates.
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode calculated totals, growth rates, or averages that should be formulas.
Do not save a workbook opened with data-only mode if formulas must be preserved.
Do not ignore formula errors after adding or changing workbook references.

faq.question: What file types does this skill support?
faq.answer: It focuses on Excel .xlsx files and CSV-based spreadsheet workflows.

faq.question: Does it calculate formulas by itself?
faq.answer: The skill uses LibreOffice through recalc.py to recalculate formulas after workbook changes.

faq.question: Can it preserve an existing template?
faq.answer: Yes. The instructions emphasize matching existing formatting, style, and workbook conventions.

faq.question: Can it find formula errors?
faq.answer: Yes. The recalculation workflow scans cells for common Excel errors and reports affected locations.

faq.question: Is LibreOffice required?
faq.answer: LibreOffice is required for the provided formula recalculation script.

faq.question: Can it replace Microsoft Excel?
faq.answer: No. It automates workbook creation, editing, and validation, but advanced Excel-specific behavior may still need manual review.
